title: Enable flux-operator Web UI in kind overlay
description:
# GitHub Issue #257: Enable the flux-operator Web UI in the kind overlay and document access in the Quick Start

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Enable the flux-operator's embedded Flux Web UI in the kind overlay as an additional demo surface alongside Headlamp (`deploy/kind/base/headlamp.yaml`) and the OpenBao UI (`deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:42-57`), and add a Quick Start "Step 4c" section that covers port-forward + access. The Web UI is shipped by ControlPlane as part of the same `flux-operator` chart that CC-0085 already bootstraps (`hack/deploy-infra.sh:58,701` — `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION=\"v0.47.0\"`, installed via `kubectl apply -f .../install.yaml`), and since the operator is installed from `install.yaml` rather than Helm, its `web.enabled` Helm value stays at the default (off). That matches the documented prerequisite for the standalone install path (\"Flux Operator configured with web UI disabled (`web.enabled: false`)\" — [fluxoperator.dev/docs/web-ui/standalone-install](https://fluxoperator.dev/docs/web-ui/standalone-install/)), which deploys the UI as a separate `HelmRelease` driven by a `ResourceSet` that pulls the `flux-operator` chart from `oci://ghcr.io/controlplaneio-fluxcd/charts/flux-operator` with `web.serverOnly: true` and `installCRDs: false`.

Concrete steps: (1) add a new manifest `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` containing the upstream `ResourceSet/flux-web` from the standalone-install docs page — `apiVersion: fluxcd.controlplane.io/v1`, `kind: ResourceSet`, `metadata.namespace: flux-system`, inputs pinning `version` (SemVer — `\"0.47.x\"` to track the same minor as `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION`), resources are the nested `OCIRepository` + `HelmRelease/flux-web` templated from those inputs, `spec.values.web.serverOnly: true`, `values.installCRDs: false`, `values.fullnameOverride: flux-web`; (2) reference the file from `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:15-18` (the same `resources` list that already adds `headlamp.yaml`); (3) add a new Quick Start "Step 4c — Open the Flux Web UI" section next to "Step 4 — Open the Headlamp UI" (`docs/quick-start.md:175-213`) and "Step 4b — Open the OpenBao UI" (`docs/quick-start.md:217`) documenting the exact `kubectl port-forward svc/flux-web -n flux-system 9080:9080` call and the `http://localhost:9080` URL; (4) update the text at `docs/quick-start.md:196-200` that currently says \"the Quick Start does not enable it\" to point at the new Step 4c; (5) extend the `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` assertions from CC-0085 to also verify that `HelmRelease/flux-web` (or whichever final release name is chosen) reaches `Ready=True` in the kind cluster, and that `Deployment/flux-web` / `Service/flux-web` exist on port 9080 — same shape as the existing five flux-system controller deployment assertions. Pin the `ResourceSet` input `version` with a Renovate `customManager` entry analogous to the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` manager that CC-0085 added (`renovate.json` custom manager regex for `hack/deploy-infra.sh`), so the UI tracks the flux-operator minor automatically.

**Motivation**: The Quick Start right now explicitly *names* the Web UI and then turns it off — `docs/quick-start.md:196-200` says \"The \`flux-operator\` itself also ships an embedded Flux Web UI ([fluxoperator.dev/web-ui](https://fluxoperator.dev/web-ui/)); the Quick Start does not enable it — the \`FluxInstance\` in \`deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml\` has no web-UI configuration — so all Flux state is viewed through Headlamp here.\" That framing was right when `flux-operator` had just landed in CC-0085 and adding a second Flux UI on top of Headlamp felt like scope creep, but it leaves two weaknesses in the on-ramp: (a) contributors reading the Quick Start get pointed at `fluxoperator.dev/web-ui/` but then have no local way to reach it, so the link is decorative — and the standalone-install docs page makes enabling it a 30-line ResourceSet, no FluxInstance surgery required; (b) Headlamp's Flux plugin covers \"Helm Releases / Kustomizations / Sources / Flux Runtime\" (`docs/quick-start.md:204-209`) but does *not* render `ResourceSet` / `ResourceSetInputProvider` / `FluxReport` as first-class panes with reconciliation triggers, and those are exactly the surfaces the flux-operator introduces on top of plain Flux — the native Web UI is the only UI that does. Enabling it in kind also gets ahead of the question that will show up the moment someone asks \"we're running flux-operator, where's the dashboard?\" — today the Quick Start answer is \"Headlamp, kind of\"; after this change it is \"both, and here is the difference\". Scope and shape are the same as CC-0082 (OpenBao UI flip, issue #246) and the Headlamp onboarding: kind-only, one manifest, one Quick Start section — production `deploy/flux-system/` stays untouched because exposing a Flux dashboard in production requires TLS + SSO per the upstream docs (\"External exposure requires configuring Ingress or Gateway API resources, with mandatory TLS encryption and Single Sign-On authentication\"), which is a much larger conversation tied to the existing HTTPRoute sub-reconciler (CC-0065) and OIDC plumbing.

**Affected Areas**:
- `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` (new — `ResourceSet/flux-web` in the `flux-system` namespace with inline inputs, `web.serverOnly: true`, `installCRDs: false`, chart version pin tracking `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION`)
- `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` (add `flux-web.yaml` to `resources` at line 15-18 alongside `headlamp.yaml`; production overlay at `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` stays unchanged — kind-only flip, same posture as Headlamp)
- `docs/quick-start.md` (new \"Step 4c — Open the Flux Web UI\" section between the existing Step 4 (Headlamp) and Step 4b (OpenBao UI); rewrite the \"Accessing the Flux UI\" paragraph at lines 196-200 to point at Step 4c instead of saying the UI is not enabled; extend the \"Architecture snapshot\" block (`docs/quick-start.md:141-155`) to list the `flux-web` Deployment the same way it lists Headlamp)
- `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` (document the new `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` in the same shape as the kind-overlay entries for `headlamp.yaml` and the OpenBao UI patch; note the Renovate pin)
- `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` (extend the existing CC-0085 flux-system assertions so that on kind clusters the `flux-web` `HelmRelease` reports `Ready=True` and the `Deployment`/`Service` are healthy — gate on the kind overlay only if the assertion would otherwise run in a production overlay context)
- `renovate.json` (add a `customManagers` entry for the `ResourceSet` `version` input or wire it into the existing `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` manager so UI chart bumps follow operator bumps automatically)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` contains a `ResourceSet/flux-web` (namespace `flux-system`) matching the upstream standalone-install manifest at [fluxoperator.dev/docs/web-ui/standalone-install](https://fluxoperator.dev/docs/web-ui/standalone-install/), with `spec.inputs` pinning `version` to a SemVer range that tracks the same minor as `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` (e.g. `0.47.x`), `installCRDs: false`, `web.serverOnly: true`, and `fullnameOverride: flux-web`
- [ ] `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` references the new file; production `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` is **not** modified
- [ ] `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster ends with `HelmRelease/flux-web` (namespace `flux-system`) `Ready=True` within the existing `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT` window and `Deployment/flux-web` reporting `Available=True`
- [ ] `kubectl port-forward svc/flux-web -n flux-system 9080:9080` followed by `curl -sf http://localhost:9080/` returns HTTP 200 with a non-empty HTML body
- [ ] `docs/quick-start.md` gains a \"Step 4c — Open the Flux Web UI\" section modelled on Step 4b, covering the port-forward command, the `http://localhost:9080` URL, a short note on what the UI adds on top of Headlamp (ResourceSet / FluxReport / InputProvider panes — the flux-operator-specific surfaces Headlamp's Flux plugin does not render), and a note that the production overlay keeps it disabled
- [ ] `docs/quick-start.md:196-200` is updated to replace the \"the Quick Start does not enable it\" sentence with a pointer at the new Step 4c
- [ ] The \"What gets deployed\" details block in `docs/quick-start.md` lists `flux-system   flux-web-*   Ready` alongside the existing controllers
- [ ] `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` documents the new manifest in the same shape as the other kind-overlay entries
- [ ] `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` asserts `HelmRelease/flux-web` Ready and the backing `Deployment`/`Service` exist on port 9080 when running on the kind overlay; the assertion does not break when the chainsaw test is run against a cluster that does not carry the kind overlay
- [ ] `renovate.json` has a `customManagers` entry that keeps the `ResourceSet` chart version pin in sync with flux-operator releases, with auto-merge for minor/patch bumps consistent with the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` manager added in CC-0085
- [ ] `.github/workflows/*.yml` — no direct workflow changes expected; the existing e2e CI job picks the new HelmRelease up through the chainsaw extension above

**Non-Goals**:
- Enabling the Flux Web UI in production (`deploy/flux-system/`). External exposure requires TLS + SSO per upstream docs, which the project does not yet have a story for on the Flux control plane (the Keystone HTTPRoute sub-reconciler from CC-0065 is unrelated). A production UI is a separate conversation covering Ingress/Gateway API, OIDC integration, and RBAC hardening — out of scope here, same way the OpenBao UI flip (#246) explicitly stayed kind-only.
- Enabling the UI inline via `FluxInstance.spec.web.enabled` (or the flux-operator Helm chart's `web.enabled` value). The operator today is installed via `kubectl apply -f install.yaml` (CC-0085, `hack/deploy-infra.sh:700-702`), so the Helm-values path is not available without switching the bootstrap to Helm — a much larger change. The standalone `ResourceSet` path described on the upstream docs page is exactly the supported alternative when the operator is managed this way.
- Replacing Headlamp. The Headlamp Flux plugin remains the generic Kubernetes-resource browser; the Flux Web UI is a second, Flux-specific surface that renders flux-operator-native kinds (`ResourceSet`, `ResourceSetInputProvider`, `FluxReport`) that Headlamp does not. Both stay shipped in the kind overlay — deliberately, same as the OpenBao UI coexisting with `kubectl exec`-based access.
- Exposing the Web UI through Ingress/LoadBalancer/Gateway API in kind. Port-forward is the convention every other UI in the Quick Start uses (Headlamp, OpenBao, Keystone API), and this follows it.
- Wiring any authentication on the kind Web UI. The Quick Start cluster is single-user localhost-only, identical posture to Headlamp (`kubectl create token` + port-forward) and OpenBao (root token + port-forward).

**References**:
- Standalone install guide: https://fluxoperator.dev/docs/web-ui/standalone-install/
- Flux Web UI overview: https://fluxoperator.dev/web-ui/
- flux-operator chart (OCI): `oci://ghcr.io/controlplaneio-fluxcd/charts/flux-operator`
- Prior art for kind-only UI additions: #246 (OpenBao UI, CC-0082)
- Upstream bootstrap landed in: #254 (CC-0085 — flux-operator + `FluxInstance`)

**Labels:** enhancement

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/257

stories.title: Contributor can open the flux-operator native Web UI in kind
stories.want: a port-forward + URL that reaches the Flux Web UI served by the flux-operator on the kind cluster
stories.so_that: I can browse flux-operator-native surfaces (ResourceSet, ResourceSetInputProvider, FluxReport) that Headlamp's Flux plugin does not render as first-class panes (CC-0086)
criteria: `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster installs the flux-web `HelmRelease` to `Ready=True` within `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT` (CC-0086, REQ-001, REQ-003)
`kubectl port-forward svc/flux-web -n flux-system 9080:9080` exposes the UI locally and `curl -sf http://localhost:9080/` returns HTTP 200 with a non-empty HTML body (CC-0086, REQ-010)
The Quick Start Step 4c section documents the exact port-forward command, the `http://localhost:9080` URL, and what the UI adds on top of Headlamp (CC-0086, REQ-005)
The Step 4c section explicitly notes that the production overlay keeps the Web UI disabled — same posture as the OpenBao UI flip (CC-0086, REQ-005)

stories.title: Platform maintainer keeps the Quick Start internally consistent
stories.want: the existing Headlamp-section paragraph that currently says 'the Quick Start does not enable it' rewritten to point at the new Step 4c, and the 'What gets deployed' block updated to list `flux-web`
stories.so_that: readers do not bounce between contradictory statements about whether the native Web UI is available (CC-0086)
criteria: `docs/quick-start.md:196-200` no longer claims the Flux Web UI is unavailable; it points at the new Step 4c anchor (CC-0086, REQ-006)
The `::: details What gets deployed` block lists `flux-system   flux-web-*   Ready` in the same shape as the existing controller rows (CC-0086, REQ-007)
No other Quick Start step's workflow is altered — the change is additive and localised to the Flux UI paragraphs (CC-0086, REQ-006)
`markdownlint` / cross-reference anchors resolve: Step 4c has a stable `{#step-4c-flux-web-ui}` anchor in the same style as `{#step-4b-openbao-ui}` (CC-0086, REQ-005)

stories.title: Operator reviewer finds the new kind-only manifest documented in the reference
stories.want: `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` to describe `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` — what it deploys, which namespace, which chart version pin, and that it is kind-only
stories.so_that: the reference matches reality and the kind-only posture of the manifest is explicit (CC-0086)
criteria: A new subsection under a Kind-only Demo Addons heading (or equivalent) describes the flux-web manifest: API version, name, namespace, chart source, pinned version input, and `web.serverOnly: true` (CC-0086, REQ-008)
The entry notes the Renovate `customManager` that keeps the chart input pin aligned with `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` (CC-0086, REQ-004, REQ-008)
The entry explicitly states the production overlay `deploy/flux-system/` does not reference this file (CC-0086, REQ-002, REQ-008)
The SPDX header block of the new manifest file follows the project convention (3-line Apache-2.0) so the reference description matches (CC-0086, REQ-001)

stories.title: CI proves the Web UI is healthy end-to-end
stories.want: the existing `infra-stack-health` chainsaw suite to also assert the flux-web HelmRelease, Deployment, and Service health
stories.so_that: a broken UI chart bump is caught by the same gate that already guards the five flux-system controller Deployments (CC-0086)
criteria: Chainsaw asserts `HelmRelease/flux-web` in `flux-system` reaches `Ready=True` (CC-0086, REQ-009)
Chainsaw asserts `Deployment/flux-web` in `flux-system` has `availableReplicas > 0` (CC-0086, REQ-009)
Chainsaw asserts `Service/flux-web` in `flux-system` exposes TCP port `9080` (CC-0086, REQ-009)

The new assertions execute within the existing `timeouts.assert: 5m` envelope and do not break a run against a cluster that does not carry the kind overlay — either by moving the flux-web block into a dedicated step that can be skipped, or by keeping the chainsaw suite bound to kind-overlay CI only (CC-0086, REQ-011)

stories.title: Renovate keeps the Web UI chart pin aligned with the flux-operator release
stories.want: a Renovate `customManager` that scans the new `ResourceSet` `version` input and opens PRs when the flux-operator publishes a new minor/patch
stories.so_that: the Web UI does not drift away from the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` constant already tracked by CC-0085 (CC-0086)
criteria: `renovate.json` declares a `customManagers` entry targeting `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` with the `github-releases` datasource and `controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator` package name — or the existing flux-operator manager is extended to cover the new file (CC-0086, REQ-004)

Paired `packageRules` disable major bumps and automerge minor/patch with `minimumReleaseAge: '3 days'`, mirroring the FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION pattern that already exists in `renovate.json:48-63` (CC-0086, REQ-004)
`renovate-config-validator` accepts the resulting `renovate.json` (CC-0086, REQ-004)

A new unit test under `tests/unit/renovate/` modelled on `fluxoperator_custommanager_test.sh` captures the chart pin value from `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` via the declared `matchStrings` regex (CC-0086, REQ-004)

stories.title: Production operator keeps the status quo
stories.want: the production `deploy/flux-system/` overlay to stay unchanged — no flux-web manifest, no FluxInstance mutation, no new HelmRelease
stories.so_that: the kind-only demo flip does not leak into production, where exposing a Flux dashboard requires TLS + SSO per upstream docs (CC-0086)
criteria: `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` is not modified by this feature (CC-0086, REQ-002)
`deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` is not modified by this feature (CC-0086, REQ-002)
`kustomize build deploy/flux-system/` emits the same set of resource kinds as on `main` before this change (CC-0086, REQ-002)
The new `flux-web` `ResourceSet` appears only under `kustomize build deploy/kind/base/` — a unit test asserts this divergence (CC-0086, REQ-002)

requirements.description: The system SHALL ship a `ResourceSet/flux-web` manifest at `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` that installs the upstream `flux-operator` chart with `web.serverOnly: true`, `installCRDs: false`, and `fullnameOverride: flux-web` in the `flux-system` namespace — matching the standalone-install docs at fluxoperator.dev (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: This is the kind-only demo surface the feature adds; it must match the supported upstream shape so the flux-operator chart reconciles without modifying the existing `FluxInstance` or the `install.yaml`-based bootstrap

scenarios.name: Manifest renders through kustomize
scenarios.when: `kustomize build deploy/kind/base/` is executed on a clean checkout
scenarios.then: the output contains a `ResourceSet` named `flux-web` in the `flux-system` namespace with `spec.inputs` pinning `version` to `0.47.x` (or the minor matching `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION`) and nested resources for `OCIRepository` + `HelmRelease/flux-web`
and_then: rendered `HelmRelease` carries `values.web.serverOnly: true`
rendered `HelmRelease` carries `values.installCRDs: false`
rendered `HelmRelease` carries `values.fullnameOverride: flux-web`
rendered `OCIRepository.spec.url` equals `oci://ghcr.io/controlplaneio-fluxcd/charts/flux-operator`

scenarios.name: SPDX header is present
scenarios.when: the file `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` is inspected
scenarios.then: lines 1-3 are the standard SPDX Apache-2.0 header
and_then: the file carries a `# Feature: CC-0086, REQ-001` marker in the leading comment block

scenarios.name: Minor track tracks flux-operator minor
scenarios.when: `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` is bumped to a new minor (e.g. `v0.48.0`)
scenarios.then: the chart version input in `flux-web.yaml` SHOULD be bumped in lockstep by the Renovate customManager declared in REQ-004, not hand-edited
and_then: the pin is expressed as a SemVer range (`0.47.x`) rather than a pinned patch, so patch releases land via the chart OCI without a Renovate round-trip

requirements.description: The kind overlay `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` SHALL reference `flux-web.yaml` in its `resources` list; the production overlay `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` and the `FluxInstance` at `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` SHALL remain unchanged (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: Kind-only posture is identical to the Headlamp addon and the OpenBao UI flip — exposing a Flux dashboard in production requires TLS + SSO, which is out of scope here

scenarios.name: Kind overlay renders flux-web ResourceSet
scenarios.when: `kustomize build deploy/kind/base/` is executed
scenarios.then: the rendered output contains exactly one `ResourceSet` named `flux-web` in the `flux-system` namespace
and_then: the rendered output also still contains the existing `HelmRelease/headlamp` and the OpenBao UI patch

scenarios.name: Production overlay is unchanged
scenarios.when: `kustomize build deploy/flux-system/` is executed
scenarios.then: the rendered output contains zero `ResourceSet` resources and no reference to `flux-web`
and_then: `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` diff between this branch and `main` is empty
`deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` diff between this branch and `main` is empty

scenarios.name: Resources list insertion point
scenarios.when: `flux-web.yaml` is added to the kind kustomization
scenarios.then: it is placed next to `headlamp.yaml` in the `resources` list (lines 15-18), with an SPDX-consistent header comment explaining it is a kind-only demo addon (CC-0086)
and_then: `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base/` remains idempotent — the new ResourceSet is created once and reconciled thereafter

requirements.description: The `deploy-infra` bootstrap SHALL leave `HelmRelease/flux-web` in `flux-system` `Ready=True` within `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT`, and `Deployment/flux-web` reporting `Available=True`, on a fresh kind cluster (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: Without this guarantee the Quick Start Step 4c would race the reconciler and the chainsaw assertions from REQ-009 would flake

scenarios.name: HelmRelease reaches Ready
scenarios.when: `make deploy-infra` completes on a fresh kind cluster
scenarios.then: `kubectl get helmrelease/flux-web -n flux-system -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'` prints `True`
and_then: this is achieved without adding `flux-web` to the script's explicit `wait_for_helmreleases` list — the existing ResourceSet reconcile + HelmRelease retry loop is sufficient, same posture as Headlamp

scenarios.name: Deployment reports Available
scenarios.when: `HelmRelease/flux-web` is Ready
scenarios.then: `Deployment/flux-web -n flux-system` reports `availableReplicas > 0` within the existing 5m chainsaw `timeouts.assert` window

scenarios.name: Pod crash backs off without breaking deploy-infra
scenarios.when: an image pull fault briefly delays the Web UI pod
scenarios.then: the `deploy-infra` script does NOT fail — the flux-web HelmRelease is not in the `wait_for_helmreleases` gate, same as Headlamp; downstream chainsaw retries take the liveness assertion on their own timeline

requirements.description: `renovate.json` SHALL declare a `customManagers` entry — or extend the existing flux-operator manager — that tracks the chart version input pinned in `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml`, with paired `packageRules` that disable major bumps and automerge minor/patch with `minimumReleaseAge: '3 days'` (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: The Web UI ships from the same flux-operator chart already tracked by CC-0085; divergent pins would let the UI rot relative to the operator

scenarios.name: renovate-config-validator accepts the change
scenarios.when: `npx --yes --package renovate -- renovate-config-validator renovate.json` runs
scenarios.then: the validator exits with status 0
and_then: no warnings are emitted about unreferenced file patterns

scenarios.name: matchStrings captures the flux-web chart pin
scenarios.when: the declared `matchStrings` regex is applied to `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml`
scenarios.then: the named group `currentValue` equals the chart version string currently pinned in that file (e.g. `0.47.x`)
and_then: the `packageNameTemplate` resolves to `controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator`

scenarios.name: Package rules mirror the FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION pattern
scenarios.when: `renovate.json` is inspected
scenarios.then: there is a `packageRules` entry that disables major updates for the flux-web pin and a second entry that automerges minor/patch with `minimumReleaseAge: '3 days'`
and_then: the two rules are scoped via `matchFileNames: ['deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml']` or via `matchPackageNames: ['controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator']` combined with the existing rules

requirements.description: `docs/quick-start.md` SHALL gain a new `Step 4c — Open the Flux Web UI` section, anchored `{#step-4c-flux-web-ui}`, placed between the current Step 4 (Headlamp) and Step 4b (OpenBao UI), with the exact port-forward command, the `http://localhost:9080` URL, a short note on what the UI adds on top of Headlamp, and a note that production keeps it disabled (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: Without this section the newly-reconciled Web UI has no documented access path for contributors

scenarios.name: Section is present and accessible
scenarios.when: the Quick Start page is rendered
scenarios.then: a `## Step 4c — Open the Flux Web UI {#step-4c-flux-web-ui}` heading exists between `## Step 4 — Open the Headlamp UI` and `## Step 4b — Open the OpenBao UI`
and_then: the section contains the literal command `kubectl port-forward svc/flux-web -n flux-system 9080:9080`
the section contains the URL `http://localhost:9080`
the section lists the three flux-operator-native kinds the UI renders that Headlamp does not: `ResourceSet`, `ResourceSetInputProvider`, `FluxReport`
the section states that `deploy/flux-system/` keeps the UI disabled and links to the Non-Goals rationale (TLS + SSO prerequisites)

scenarios.name: Step 4 paragraph is rewritten
scenarios.when: `docs/quick-start.md:196-200` is read
scenarios.then: the paragraph no longer claims `the Quick Start does not enable it`; it points at the `#step-4c-flux-web-ui` anchor and says Headlamp remains the generic Kubernetes-resource browser while the native UI covers flux-operator-specific kinds
and_then: the `fluxoperator.dev/web-ui/` link is retained as the external reference for what the UI renders

scenarios.name: No unrelated Step is altered
scenarios.when: the diff of `docs/quick-start.md` is inspected
scenarios.then: only the Step 4 paragraph (lines 196-200), the new Step 4c section, and the `What gets deployed` block (REQ-007) are touched — Steps 1-3 and 5-9 are unchanged

requirements.description: The `Accessing the Flux UI` paragraph at `docs/quick-start.md:196-200` SHALL be rewritten to acknowledge that the native Web UI IS enabled in kind and to cross-link Step 4c; the `Pane` table immediately below SHALL be preserved because it still describes the Headlamp Flux plugin (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: The current sentence explicitly contradicts the new reality; readers will otherwise bounce between contradictory statements

scenarios.name: Old sentence removed
scenarios.when: the file is searched for the literal string `the Quick Start does not enable it`
scenarios.then: no match is found

scenarios.name: Cross-link present
scenarios.when: the rewritten paragraph is read
scenarios.then: it contains either `Step 4c` or the anchor `#step-4c-flux-web-ui` so readers can jump from the Headlamp section to the native UI section

scenarios.name: Headlamp pane table preserved
scenarios.when: the four-row table (`Helm Releases`, `Kustomizations`, `Sources`, `Flux Runtime`) is read
scenarios.then: the table is unchanged — it still describes the Headlamp Flux plugin; the flux-web section has its own short list of additional panes

requirements.description: The `::: details What gets deployed` block in `docs/quick-start.md` SHALL list `flux-system   flux-web-*   Ready` alongside the existing five flux-system controllers and Headlamp (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: Readers scanning the cluster inventory expect every Quick-Start-visible Deployment to appear here

scenarios.name: Entry present
scenarios.when: the `What gets deployed` details block is rendered
scenarios.then: a line `flux-system           flux-web-*                     Ready (kind-only; see Step 4c)` exists, indented consistently with the surrounding rows

scenarios.name: Location correct
scenarios.when: the block is inspected
scenarios.then: the flux-web row is placed within the existing `flux-system` group so the five-controller cluster is adjacent to it — not sprinkled into the middle of another namespace's rows

scenarios.name: No false claims on wait ordering
scenarios.when: the row is read
scenarios.then: it does not claim flux-web is part of the `deploy-infra` wait list (it is not, per REQ-003); the Starting-vs-Ready qualifier matches the Headlamp precedent

requirements.description: `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` SHALL describe `deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml` alongside the existing kind-overlay entries — identifying API version, kind, name, namespace, chart source URL, pinned input, and the kind-only posture (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: The reference doc is the single source of truth for the deploy manifest layout; unreferenced kind-overlay manifests would drift

scenarios.name: Entry exists under a Kind Overlay section
scenarios.when: the reference is rendered
scenarios.then: the document gains a `Kind Overlay Demo Addons` section (or equivalent) that describes both the existing Headlamp + OpenBao UI patch entries (if not already captured) and the new flux-web ResourceSet — API version `fluxcd.controlplane.io/v1`, kind `ResourceSet`, name `flux-web`, namespace `flux-system`, chart `oci://ghcr.io/controlplaneio-fluxcd/charts/flux-operator`
and_then: the entry notes `web.serverOnly: true` and `installCRDs: false`
the entry links at the standalone-install docs at fluxoperator.dev

scenarios.name: Renovate pin cross-referenced
scenarios.when: the entry is read
scenarios.then: it mentions the Renovate `customManager` (REQ-004) and the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` constant it aligns with

scenarios.name: Kind-only posture explicit
scenarios.when: the entry is read
scenarios.then: it states that `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` does NOT reference `flux-web.yaml` and explains why (TLS + SSO prerequisites for a production Flux dashboard)

requirements.description: `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` SHALL assert, in a step scoped to the kind overlay, that `HelmRelease/flux-web`, `Deployment/flux-web`, and `Service/flux-web` (all in `flux-system`) are healthy and that the Service exposes TCP port `9080` (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: Without a gate the Web UI can rot silently between flux-operator bumps; this catches the failure at the same point that already guards the five controller Deployments

scenarios.name: HelmRelease Ready
scenarios.when: the chainsaw step runs against a kind cluster
scenarios.then: the assertion `HelmRelease/flux-web` has `conditions[?type=='Ready'].status == 'True'` within `timeouts.assert: 5m`

scenarios.name: Deployment Available
scenarios.when: the chainsaw step runs against a kind cluster
scenarios.then: the assertion `Deployment/flux-web -n flux-system` has `availableReplicas > 0`

scenarios.name: Service exposes port 9080
scenarios.when: the chainsaw step runs against a kind cluster
scenarios.then: the assertion `Service/flux-web -n flux-system` has `spec.ports` containing an entry with `port: 9080` and `protocol: TCP`
and_then: a selector is present that targets the flux-web pods so `kubectl port-forward svc/flux-web` reaches them

requirements.description: When port-forwarded, the Flux Web UI endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with a non-empty HTML body at `http://localhost:9080/` (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: This is the end-to-end acceptance path a human actually follows; it closes the loop between manifest Ready, Service port mapping, and a working UI

scenarios.name: Local curl returns 200
scenarios.when: `kubectl port-forward svc/flux-web -n flux-system 9080:9080` is running and `curl -sf -o /tmp/body.html -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:9080/` is executed
scenarios.then: the exit status is 0, the HTTP code is 200, and `/tmp/body.html` is non-empty
and_then: the response `Content-Type` header starts with `text/html`

scenarios.name: Server-only mode serves UI assets without additional auth
scenarios.when: the UI is opened in the browser at `http://localhost:9080`
scenarios.then: the index page renders with the flux-operator branding — `serverOnly: true` does not disable the static assets, it only skips the embedded FluxInstance-sidecar coupling
and_then: no authentication dialog appears — the kind cluster is localhost-only, matching the Headlamp + OpenBao posture

scenarios.name: 404 on unknown path is the UI's own 404, not the port-forward's
scenarios.when: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:9080/does-not-exist` is executed
scenarios.then: the response code is 200 or 404 — both prove the UI handler is reachable (SPA serves index.html on deep links); the response is never a `connection refused`

requirements.description: The new chainsaw assertions SHALL NOT break a suite run against a cluster that does not carry the kind overlay — either by keeping the suite bound to kind-overlay CI only (same as today) or by placing the flux-web assertions in a dedicated step with a clear header that explains the kind-overlay gating (CC-0086)
requirements.rationale: `infra-stack-health` today already covers kind-only surfaces (Chaos Mesh patch, OpenBao `ui=true`) via the assumption that the suite is always run against the kind overlay — this change must preserve that assumption or make it explicit

scenarios.name: Kind overlay run passes
scenarios.when: `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/` is executed against a kind cluster bootstrapped by `make deploy-infra`
scenarios.then: all steps pass, including the new flux-web step

scenarios.name: Documentation matches test scope
scenarios.when: the new chainsaw step is read
scenarios.then: its header comment states it assumes the kind overlay and references CC-0086 + the relevant REQ-IDs
and_then: the step's position in the file is `Step 7` (after the existing `Step 6 flux-operator` block)

scenarios.name: Assertion timeout budget
scenarios.when: the new assertions are authored
scenarios.then: they fit inside the existing `timeouts.assert: 5m` envelope without a per-step override

implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS
-------------------------
1. Standalone ResourceSet path (not `FluxInstance.spec.web.enabled`). CC-0085 installs flux-operator via `kubectl apply -f install.yaml` rather than Helm (hack/deploy-infra.sh:58,700-702), so the operator-level `web.enabled` Helm value is unreachable without switching the bootstrap to Helm — a much larger change. The upstream standalone-install docs page explicitly documents this ResourceSet path as the supported alternative when the operator is managed this way.
2. Kind-only posture, identical to the Headlamp addon (`deploy/kind/base/headlamp.yaml`) and the OpenBao UI flip (`deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:42-57`). Production `deploy/flux-system/` stays untouched — a production Flux dashboard requires TLS + SSO per the upstream docs, which the project does not yet have a story for on the Flux control plane.
3. ResourceSet input pins `version: "0.47.x"` (SemVer minor track). Patch bumps land without a Renovate round-trip via the chart OCI; minor bumps are Renovate's responsibility, mirroring the CC-0085 FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION manager.
4. Not added to `wait_for_helmreleases` in `deploy-infra.sh`. A broken upstream chart release must not block `make deploy-infra` — same posture as Headlamp (`docs/quick-start.md:157-159`). Step 4c + the chainsaw gate in `infra-stack-health/` catch the failure where it is actionable.
5. Chainsaw Step 7 extends the existing CC-0085 flux-operator block — it does not create a new suite. The suite already assumes kind-overlay scope (the Chaos Mesh + OpenBao UI assertions depend on kind-only patches), so the new assertions inherit that assumption without special gating.
6. Renovate customManager is a NEW entry (not a merge into the existing `hack/deploy-infra.sh` manager) because the `managerFilePatterns` regex must scope to the manifest file. Both entries share the same `packageNameTemplate`, so `packageRules` that already target `controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator` apply to both without duplication.

DATA FLOW
-------------------------
`make deploy-infra` (hack/deploy-infra.sh)
  └─ Step 2: kubectl apply -f flux-operator install.yaml  (CC-0085, unchanged)
  └─ Step 2: kubectl apply -f deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml  (CC-0085, unchanged)
  └─ wait_for_fluxinstance — FluxInstance/flux Ready
  └─ Step 3: kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base/
       │
       ├─ headlamp.yaml  (CC-0086-adjacent, unchanged)
       ├─ [patches]       (CC-0086-adjacent, unchanged)
       └─ flux-web.yaml  ── NEW (CC-0086, REQ-001)
            └─ ResourceSet/flux-web (flux-system)
                 └─ materializes:
                      ├─ OCIRepository/flux-operator (chart)
                      └─ HelmRelease/flux-web
                           └─ Deployment/flux-web
                                └─ Service/flux-web :9080/tcp
  └─ Step 4..8 unchanged

Contributor path
  kubectl port-forward svc/flux-web -n flux-system 9080:9080
  └─ curl http://localhost:9080/  → HTTP 200 (REQ-010)

Renovate path
  deploy/kind/base/flux-web.yaml  ── `version: "0.47.x"` pinned
  └─ customManager (renovate.json)
       └─ matchStrings → currentValue
       └─ datasourceTemplate: github-releases
       └─ packageNameTemplate: controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator
       └─ packageRules: disable major, automerge minor/patch, minimumReleaseAge 3d

POTENTIAL PITFALLS
-------------------------
- `installCRDs: false` is NOT optional. The flux-operator chart's default install includes CRDs; because CC-0085 already installs them via `install.yaml`, letting the chart install them again causes a `failed to install CRD` apply conflict and flux-web never becomes Ready. Miss this, and REQ-003 fails.
- `fullnameOverride: flux-web`. Without it the Service/Deployment names depend on the Helm release name; the chainsaw assertions (REQ-009) and the Quick Start port-forward (REQ-005) are both hard-coded to `flux-web`. Matches the feature description verbatim.
- `web.serverOnly: true`. The chart also publishes a sidecar mode that couples the UI to the FluxInstance; `serverOnly: true` is the supported mode for the standalone install path and is what the upstream docs page instructs.
- Chart version pin as a SemVer range (`0.47.x`), not a pinned patch. A pinned patch would require a Renovate bump for every upstream patch — cheaper to let OCI pull resolve to the latest patch within the minor.
- The `ResourceSet` `metadata.namespace: flux-system` must exist. `flux-system` is created by CC-0085's FluxInstance bootstrap (`deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml`), so this is a given — but a code-reviewer who reads the manifest in isolation may flag it. The header comment should cite the provenance.
- Do NOT add `flux-web` to `wait_for_helmreleases` in `deploy-infra.sh`. The chainsaw suite catches it with a dedicated 5m budget; mixing it into the main wait gate risks blocking `make deploy-infra` on a transient upstream chart issue.

FILES / PATTERNS TO FOLLOW
-------------------------
- `deploy/kind/base/headlamp.yaml` — SPDX header, kind-only header comment, structural template for the new manifest
- `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:15-18` — resources list insertion point
- `renovate.json:19-31` + `renovate.json:48-63` — customManager + packageRules pattern for flux-operator
- `tests/unit/renovate/fluxoperator_custommanager_test.sh` — test harness pattern for customManager unit tests
- `tests/unit/deploy/flux_system_fluxinstance_test.sh` — unit-test pattern for deploy manifests using `tests/lib/assertions.sh`
- `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml:185-269` — Step 6 flux-operator assertions to model Step 7 on
- `docs/quick-start.md:175-249` — Step 4 / 4b template to model Step 4c on
- `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md:341-367` — kind-overlay doc shape (Chaos Mesh)

DEFERRED ITEMS
-------------------------
- Production Flux Web UI exposure (TLS + SSO via Ingress / Gateway API). Upstream mandates this — a separate feature that would wire OIDC into flux-operator, likely coupled to the existing HTTPRoute sub-reconciler (CC-0065). Revisit when multi-tenant production Flux is on the roadmap.
- Moving the flux-operator bootstrap from `install.yaml` to a Helm chart so `web.enabled: true` lands inline. That would retire the standalone ResourceSet path but is a larger refactor of CC-0085 — defer until another use case demands Helm-native flux-operator management.
- Integrating the Web UI link into Headlamp's sidebar via a plugin. Headlamp's Flux plugin does not today render ResourceSet/FluxReport; upstream patches would land separately.
- Replacing Headlamp with the Flux Web UI. Out of scope — they solve different problems (generic vs. Flux-specific) and the Quick Start keeps both, matching the OpenBao UI + `kubectl exec` coexistence.
